prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_travel_case departure_context_1)
(register_travel_case arrival_context_1)
(register_travel_case assessment_session_1)
(register_travel_case assessment_session_2)
(assign_validator_to_case departure_context_1 validator_instance_1)
(attach_rule_interpretation departure_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(finalize_rule_interpretation departure_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(flag_origin_requirement_for_context departure_context_1 origin_requirement_reentry_permit rule_interpretation_1)
(record_origin_document_for_requirement departure_context_1 origin_requirement_reentry_permit required_document_passport)
(reconcile_origin_requirement_submission departure_context_1 origin_requirement_reentry_permit rule_interpretation_1 required_document_passport)
(detach_rule_interpretation_from_case departure_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(assign_validator_to_case arrival_context_1 validator_instance_2)
(attach_rule_interpretation arrival_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(finalize_rule_interpretation arrival_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(flag_destination_requirement_for_context arrival_context_1 destination_requirement_visa rule_interpretation_1)
(record_destination_document_for_requirement arrival_context_1 destination_requirement_visa required_document_passport)
(reconcile_destination_requirement_submission arrival_context_1 destination_requirement_visa rule_interpretation_1 required_document_passport)
(detach_rule_interpretation_from_case arrival_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(select_and_link_template departure_context_1 arrival_context_1 origin_requirement_reentry_permit destination_requirement_visa document_pack_template_standard)
(attach_rule_interpretation departure_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(mark_template_as_ready document_pack_template_standard departure_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(approve_departure_context_assessment departure_context_1 document_pack_template_standard)
(approve_arrival_context_assessment arrival_context_1 document_pack_template_standard)
(attach_evidence_and_confirm_eligibility departure_context_1 evidence_document_insurance rule_interpretation_1)
(allocate_pack_for_departure_context departure_context_1 validator_instance_1 evidence_document_insurance)
(detach_rule_interpretation_from_case departure_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(attach_rule_interpretation arrival_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(attach_evidence_and_confirm_eligibility arrival_context_1 evidence_document_insurance rule_interpretation_1)
(allocate_pack_for_arrival_context arrival_context_1 validator_instance_2 evidence_document_insurance)
(detach_rule_interpretation_from_case arrival_context_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(assign_validator_to_case assessment_session_1 validator_instance_3)
(attach_rule_interpretation assessment_session_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(finalize_rule_interpretation assessment_session_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(attach_country_form_to_session assessment_session_1 country_form_consulate document_pack_template_standard)
(activate_country_form_in_session assessment_session_1 country_form_consulate document_pack_template_standard rule_interpretation_1)
(assign_authority_to_case assessment_session_1 authority_stakeholder_1)
(attach_verification_check_to_session assessment_session_1 verification_check_biometric)
(attach_legal_reference_to_session assessment_session_1 legal_reference_public_health_act)
(initiate_format_and_verification_checks assessment_session_1 verification_check_biometric authority_stakeholder_1 country_form_consulate document_pack_template_standard)
(initiate_legal_reference_checks assessment_session_1 legal_reference_public_health_act country_form_consulate document_pack_template_standard)
(finalize_assessment_session assessment_session_1)
(attach_evidence_and_confirm_eligibility assessment_session_1 evidence_document_insurance rule_interpretation_1)
(allocate_pack_for_session assessment_session_1 validator_instance_3 evidence_document_insurance)
(release_authority_from_case assessment_session_1 authority_stakeholder_1)
(detach_verification_check_from_session assessment_session_1 verification_check_biometric)
(detach_legal_reference_from_session assessment_session_1 legal_reference_public_health_act)
(detach_rule_interpretation_from_case assessment_session_1 rule_interpretation_1)
(assign_validator_to_case assessment_session_2 validator_instance_1)
(attach_rule_interpretation assessment_session_2 rule_interpretation_1)
(finalize_rule_interpretation assessment_session_2 rule_interpretation_1)
(attach_country_form_to_session assessment_session_2 country_form_health document_pack_template_standard)
(activate_country_form_in_session assessment_session_2 country_form_health document_pack_template_standard rule_interpretation_1)
(assign_authority_to_case assessment_session_2 authority_stakeholder_1)
(attach_verification_check_to_session assessment_session_2 verification_check_biometric)
(attach_legal_reference_to_session assessment_session_2 legal_reference_public_health_act)
(initiate_format_and_verification_checks assessment_session_2 verification_check_biometric authority_stakeholder_1 country_form_health document_pack_template_standard)
(initiate_legal_reference_checks assessment_session_2 legal_reference_public_health_act country_form_health document_pack_template_standard)
(finalize_assessment_session assessment_session_2)
(attach_evidence_and_confirm_eligibility assessment_session_2 evidence_document_insurance rule_interpretation_1)
(allocate_pack_for_session assessment_session_2 validator_instance_1 evidence_document_insurance)
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex, but I need to find a plan to achieve the goal: pack allocated for departure_context_1, arrival_context_1, assessment_session_1, and assessment_session_2.


## Planning task execution  
- The goal is to pack for contexts: departure_context_1, arrival_context_1, and two assessment sessions (assessment_session_1, assessment_session_2).  
- Need to develop a plan to ensure all necessary items are allocated for each context.  
- Focus on understanding requirements for each context to prioritize packing effectively.  
- Consider overlapping needs across contexts to streamline the packing process.

## Planning task execution  
- Departure_context_1 and arrival_context_1 are shared by both assessment sessions, suggesting a unified approach for packing.  
- Contexts and sessions have different regulatory requirements, like consulate forms for session1 and health forms for session2.  

## Identifying prerequisites  
- Packing requires eligibility_confirmed, which depends on assessment_approved for each travel case, including contexts and sessions.  
- Initially, no travel cases are registered, so start by registering departure_context_1, arrival_context_1, assessment_session_1, and assessment_session_2.  

## Managing resources  
- Three validators are available for four cases, but they can be reused after pack allocation, enabling flexible assignment.  
- Only one rule_interpretation_1 is available, needed for actions requiring assessment_rule_interpretation across all cases.  

## Navigating dependencies  
- Assessment_approved requires conditions like departure_ready and departure_requirement_confirmed, which involve verifying origin requirements or recording documents.  
- Template selection for contexts depends on readiness, creating interconnected steps between contexts and sessions.

## Exploring template selection  
- Template selection for contexts requires readiness, with actions like select_template_with_departure_context needing both departure and arrival contexts ready.  
- Sessions link to templates via session_template_link, but templates are not tied to sessions during initial selection, focusing on shared contexts.

## Processing context requirements  
- Start by registering and processing departure_context_1, assigning a validator and attaching rule_interpretation_1 to begin.  
- Use actions like flag_origin_requirement_for_context and record_origin_document_for_requirement to achieve departure_ready and departure_requirement_confirmed for shared contexts.  

## Managing rule interpretation  
- Only one rule_interpretation_1 is available, which gets consumed by attach_rule_interpretation, posing a challenge for processing multiple contexts.  
- Detaching rule_interpretation_1 from a context can make it available again, but careful sequencing is needed to handle shared resources.

## Continuing rule management  
- Rule_interpretation_1 (RI) is detached from arrival_context_1, making it available for reuse.  
- Both departure_context_1 and arrival_context_1 are now flagged, confirmed, and documented, meeting template selection prerequisites.  

## Selecting and linking templates  
- Selected and linked document_pack_template_standard to both contexts, associating origin_reentry and destination_visa requirements.  
- Template selection removes flags but isn't available for further use, requiring readiness actions like re-attaching RI.  

## Preparing templates  
- Re-attached RI to departure_context_1 to mark document_pack_template_standard as ready, fulfilling template_ready condition.  
- Marked template as ready for both contexts, enabling assessment approval for departure_context_1 and arrival_context_1.  

## Confirming eligibility  
- Attached evidence_document_insurance to departure_context_1, confirming eligibility and allocating a pack, releasing evidence afterward.  
- For arrival_context_1, RI needs detaching from departure_context_1 first, with evidence availability restored post-allocation for further steps.

## Ensuring session readiness  
- Session checks need to start with actions like initiate_legal_reference_checks, requiring session_format_ready and legal/country_form availability.  
- Found session_format_ready can be achieved through initiate_format_and_verification_checks, setting up for legal reference checks.  

## Initiating checks process  
- Initiate_legal_reference_checks sets session_checks_started without completing, meeting finalize_assessment_session’s preconditions.  
- Other advance actions set both started and completed, but we need checks_started without completion to finalize.  

## Managing check completion  
- Finalize_assessment_session requires checks_started and not checks_completed, leading to session finalization and approval.  
- This path avoids needing additional testing or clauses, leveraging existing session links and confirmations.

## Assigning resources  
- Assigned validator_instance_3 and rule_interpretation_1 to assessment_session_1, marking it under review.  
- Attached country_form_consulate to session, linking it to document_pack_template_standard and confirming template readiness.  

## Activating session components  
- Activated country_form in session, confirming format with rule_interpretation_1, setting format_confirmed.  
- Assigned authority_stakeholder_1, attached verification_check_biometric and legal_reference_public_health_act, all now unavailable for session1.  

## Initiating session checks  
- Initiated format and verification checks, setting format_ready with authority and country form linked.  
- Started legal reference checks, marking checks_started without completion, enabling session finalization.  

## Finalizing and approving  
- Finalized assessment_session_1, approving it since checks started but not completed.  
- Attached evidence_document_insurance, confirmed eligibility, and allocated pack, releasing evidence afterward.  

## Preparing for session2  
- Noted resources like authority and verification check are tied to session1, requiring release for session2.  
- Registered assessment_session_2, planning to assign available validator_instance_1 for continuation.

## Sequencing resource allocation  
- Assigned validator_instance_1 to departure_context_1, processing it sequentially to ensure resource availability.  
- Planned to reuse validator_instance_1 for session2 after releasing it from departure, optimizing efficiency.  

## Registering travel contexts  
- Registered departure_context_1 and assigned validator_instance_2 to arrival_context_1, following a sequential order.  
- Linked rule_interpretation_1 to both contexts, finalizing interpretations for origin and destination requirements.  

## Finalizing context assessments  
- Flagged and reconciled requirements like reentry permit and visa, using required_document_passport for both contexts.  
- Approved both departure and arrival context assessments after marking document_pack_template_standard as ready.  

## Managing rule interpretations  
- Detached rule_interpretation_1 from cases after use, ensuring availability for subsequent steps.  
- Reattached rule_interpretation_1 to departure_context_1 for eligibility checks, maintaining continuity.

## Managing rule interpretations  
- Detached rule_interpretation_1 from arrival_context_1 after use, freeing it for subsequent tasks.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
